Computer-Assisted Reporting Newsletter, 1990–2007

Picture this

John Sullivan NICAR staff September 1996
Journalism & NewsroomInvestigative MethodsTechnology

If you're a broadcast journalist looking to start a computer-assisted reporting program at your station but can't get support from the higher-ups, you're not alone...

If you're a broadcast journalist looking to start a computer-assisted reporting program at your station but can't get support from the higher-ups, you're not alone... Both Cox and Wendland agree that if management does not push you onto the dance floor, you need to start moving and shaking on your own. Do whatever you can to get your hands on the equipment, even if it means going over to accounting and using what they have.
